iti37:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti37:2.1:
“Endowed with two things, monks,
a monk lives full of ease in the here-&-now and is appropriately aroused for the ending of the effluents.
iti37:2.3: A sense of urgency toward things that should inspire urgency and, feeling urgency, appropriate exertion.
iti37:2.4:
Endowed with these two things,
a monk lives full of ease in the here-&-now and is appropriately aroused for the ending of the effluents.”
iti37:3.2: toward what should inspire urgency, the wise,
iti37:4.4: would attain the ending of suffering & stress.
